short_description_of_the_course.label: Short Description of the Course
short_description_of_the_course.value: This course will cover computational approaches to working with numerical data on a large scale. Computation on arrays of continuous variables underpins machine learning, information retrieval, data analytics, computer vision and signal processing. This course will cover vectorised operations on numerical arrays, fundamental stochastic and probabilistic methods and scientific visualisation.

timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.label: Timetable and length and frequency of teaching sessions
timetable_and_length_and_frequency_of_te.value: Two one-hour lectures and one one-hour tutorial per week.

course_aims.label: Course Aims
course_aims.value: This course will give students skills in manipulating continuous data, specifying problems in a forms that can be solved numerically, dealing with unreliable and uncertain information, and communicating these results. DF(H) will introduce operations on vectors and matrices, specifying and solving problems via numerical optimisation, time series modelling, scientific visualisation and basic probabilistic computation. Students will learn how to bridge the continuous and discrete worlds; for example solving graph flow problems via matrix operations, or how to do robust A/B testing of websites The focus will be firmly on applying and solving these problems from a computer science perspective and not on theoretical background or formal proofs. The course also aims to develop students’ understanding of sustainability and decarbonisation as related to the topic.

intended_learning_outcomes_of_course.label: Intended Learning Outcomes of Course

description_of_summative_assessment.label: Description of Summative Assessment
description_of_summative_assessment.value:
Examination 80% and Practical Skills Assessment 15%, in-class quizzes 5%.

Practical: Lab work, submitting code for a series of 4 laboratory exercises. Each will be worth 3.75% of the total marks for this course.
Quizzes: multiple choice questions administered during lectures.

formative_assessment_feedback.label: Formative Assessment & Feedback
formative_assessment_feedback.value: Written feedback to be provided with the five summative assessments throughout the course; verbal feedback in class sessions.
